svg.style: background: #ffffff; background-color: light-dark(#ffffff, #121212); color-scheme: light dark;

div.font: 1. SHA family: - SHA-256 is a part of sha-family 2
Here 256 means: - it generates hash of 64 hex char, which is equal to32 byte = 32 * 8 = 256 bits

switch.text: 1. SHA family: - SHA-256 is a part of sha-family...

div.div:
Anything encrypted with Private Key → can ONLY be decrypted by Public Key
Anything encrypted with Public Key → can ONLY be decrypted by Private Key

switch.text: Anything encrypted with Private Key → can ONLY be decrypted by Public Key...

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 548px; height: 1px; padding-top: 500px; margin-left: 941px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: "Helvetica"; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div:
a person also modify/temper the document and calculate new hash signature of that document and then add that new hash in that document.
This where the concept of public and private key comes in, now we have even encrypt the hash of the document also using private key, then that hash can only decrypted using public key, and if the hash is decrypt successfully, this will prove that the hash is not modified(incase if the encrypted hash is modified then the public key won,t be able to decrypt the encrypted hash, thus proving the temperness in the encrypted hash), and now once we have the authentic hash, we also correctly verify document hash with this decrypted hash after independently calculating the hash of the document also

switch.text: a person also modify/temper the document and calculate new hash signature of that document a...

a.text: Text is not SVG - cannot display
